pageTitle: Free QR Code Generator - Wi-Fi, Business Card & URL QR Codes | GetNiftyKit
metaDescription: Create a QR code for a URL, Wi-Fi network, business card, email, phone number, SMS, event, or location — free, no sign-up. Download as PNG or SVG instantly.
softwareAppDescription: A free web tool that instantly generates a QR code for a URL, Wi-Fi network, business card, email, phone number, and more — downloadable as PNG or SVG.
subtitle: From a URL to Wi-Fi and business cards — generate the right QR code for free
taglineExtra: Wi-Fi sharing, business card QR codes, event invites — create free QR codes and download them as PNG or SVG.
inputLabel: What should the QR code contain?
inputPlaceholder: Enter text or a URL (e.g. https://example.com)
infoIntroH2: What is a QR code generator?
infoIntroP: This free QR code generator instantly creates the right QR code for your purpose — a URL, Wi-Fi login details, a business card (contact), email, phone number, SMS, calendar event, or map location. Whether you need a Wi-Fi password QR code, a QR code for a business card, or one for a menu, poster, or slide deck, you can create it right here with no software to install. Every QR code can be downloaded for free as a PNG or SVG file, and everything is processed instantly in your browser.
infoHowtoP: First choose the type of QR code you need (URL, Text, Wi-Fi, Contact, Email, Phone, SMS, Event, or Location), then fill in the matching fields. A live preview of the QR code appears below as you type. Once you're happy with the result, click the "PNG" or "SVG" button to download it. If your input is too long to fit in a QR code, you'll see a message letting you know.
infoUseCases: Quickly sharing a Wi-Fi password or website address
Adding a QR code to a business card or flyer
Linking a printed menu to an online ordering page
Embedding a QR code in a presentation or poster
Adding a calendar QR code to an event invitation
Creating a QR code that starts a phone call or text message

faq.a: Yes. You can create and download QR codes for free, with no account required.
Both PNG and SVG. PNG works great for documents and printouts, while SVG is a scalable vector format that stays crisp at any size.

faq.q: Is there a limit to how much text I can encode?
faq.a: QR codes can only hold so much data. If your text is too long, you'll see a message on screen — try a shorter URL or trimming the text.

faq.q: Can I make a QR code for Wi-Fi or a business card?
faq.a: Yes. Pick Wi-Fi, Contact, Email, Phone, SMS, Event, or Location from the category tabs at the top to create a QR code for that specific purpose. For example, a Wi-Fi QR code will connect the device to that network as soon as it's scanned.

privacyNote: This tool generates QR codes entirely in your browser. Your input is never sent to or stored on a server.
